reference: Palpitations or a fast heartbeat usually develop when there are abnormalities in pulse generation or conduction in the heart. They can hinder daily activities and routine work. Sometimes, only a hollow feeling occurs without actual heart disease. You need to have an electrocardiogram during the episode if possible, or a twenty-four-hour Holter monitor recording to confirm any rhythm disturbance. If there is a disturbance, you will require a beta-blocker.
text: Palpitations or a fast heartbeat usually develop when there are abnormalities in pulse generation or conduction in the heart. They can hinder daily activities and routine work. Sometimes only a hollow feeling occurs without actual heart disease. You need to have an electrocardiogram during the episode if possible, or a 24-hour Holter monitor recording to confirm any rhythm disturbance. If there is a disturbance, you will require a beta blocker.
